resolutions.action: Reframed the CI/CD recommendation as explicit practitioner guidance rather than an evidence-backed finding, and added a sentence on recommended test pyramid composition to make it actionable.

Revised the George & Williams (2003) evidence table entry to clarify that '18% more passing test cases' refers to external black-box acceptance test pass rates on delivered software, not an internal test count metric. Also noted the study found no significant productivity loss to present a complete picture.

Replaced the inline footer citation with a dedicated References section using APA 7th edition format including DOIs and full bibliographic details. The existing sources were expanded with complete metadata; two additional post-2010 sources were added per comment 5.

Added two scoping sentences to the Introduction clarifying that the report covers general-purpose software contexts (enterprise, web, and desktop applications) and explicitly notes exclusions for safety-critical or embedded domains where TDD practices and evidence differ.

Added two post-2010 entries to the evidence table — Rafique & Mišić (2013), a 27-study meta-analysis, and Fucci et al. (2016), a multi-site blind replication that yielded null results — to broaden the evidence base and present a balanced view including mixed findings. Updated the analysis paragraph to acknowledge heterogeneity.

Replaced the vague 'consistent quality improvement' characterization of Madeyski (2010) in the evidence table with a more specific description of the meta-analysis scope and key finding. Note: exact effect size figures from the monograph were not available for inline citation; the entry reflects the study's reported directional conclusions and scope.
Updated the Executive Summary to replace '40–80%' with '40–90%' to match the Nagappan et al. (2008) finding cited consistently in Section 3 and the evidence table.
